title: At the Post Office
summary: A customer visits the post office to send a parcel overseas and buy stamps. The clerk helps him choose the right service, weigh the parcel, and complete the transaction.

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Good morning! How can I help you today?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Hi, I need to send this parcel to my family in Vietnam. I'm not sure which service to use.

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: No worries, I can help you with that. Can you pop it on the scales for me?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Right, so it weighs one point two kilograms. Would you like standard international post or express?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Standard takes about ten to fourteen business days and costs twenty-two dollars. Express gets there in three to five business days and it's thirty-eight dollars.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: I'd like it to arrive before the end of the month, so I'll go with express please.

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Good choice. I'll need you to fill in this customs declaration form. Just write a brief description of what's inside and the approximate value.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: It's mostly clothes and some chocolates. Probably worth about forty dollars altogether.

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Perfect. Just write that down here. And is there anything fragile, liquid, or battery-operated inside?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Great. While I process this, would you like to buy any stamps as well?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Yes please. Can I get a book of ten stamps for local letters?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Of course. That's twelve dollars for the stamps and thirty-eight for the parcel — fifty dollars in total. Cash or card?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Card please. Here you go. Thank you for your help!

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: No problem! Your parcel will arrive in three to five business days. Have a lovely day!

annotations.exampleEn: She wrapped the parcel carefully before taking it to the post office.

annotations.meaningVi: đặt lên cân (cách nói thân mật)
annotations.exampleEn: Can you pop the parcel on the scales so I can weigh it?

annotations.meaningVi: ngày làm việc (không tính thứ bảy, chủ nhật, ngày lễ)
annotations.exampleEn: Your order will be delivered within five business days.
He sent the documents by express post to ensure they arrived on time.
You must fill in a customs declaration form for any parcel sent abroad.
Please state the approximate value of the items in the package.
The box was labelled fragile because it contained glass items.
Battery-operated devices cannot be sent in checked luggage.

annotations.meaningVi: xử lý (một giao dịch, tài liệu, v.v.)
annotations.exampleEn: It takes a few minutes to process the payment.
That comes to eighty dollars in total.

grammarNotes.title: Need to + infinitive for necessity
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Need to + động từ nguyên thể' diễn tả điều cần thiết phải làm. 'I need to send this parcel.' — Tôi cần gửi bưu kiện này. 'I'll need you to fill in this customs declaration form.' — Tôi cần bạn điền vào tờ khai hải quan này. 'Need to' mạnh hơn 'should' nhưng nhẹ hơn 'must'. Dạng phủ định là 'don't need to' (không cần thiết phải), không phải 'needn't to'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'need to + infinitive' to express that something is necessary. 'I need to send this parcel.' / 'I'll need you to fill in this form.' Negative: 'You don't need to rush.' — it is not necessary. Compare with 'must not', which means it is forbidden: 'You must not put batteries in the parcel.' Also note 'I'll need you to do something' = I am asking you to do something; it is polite but indicates a requirement.

grammarNotes.title: Reported questions: 'What's the difference?' — asking for clarification
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong giao tiếp hàng ngày, chúng ta thường dùng các câu hỏi ngắn để xin giải thích thêm. 'What's the difference?' — Sự khác biệt là gì? 'I'm not sure which service to use.' — Tôi không chắc dùng dịch vụ nào. Đây là cách lịch sự để hỏi khi bạn không hiểu hoặc cần thêm thông tin trước khi ra quyết định. Các câu hỏi này rất thực dụng trong các tình huống dịch vụ như bưu điện, ngân hàng hay nhà thuốc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Short clarifying questions like 'What's the difference?' are very natural in service situations. Other useful patterns from this dialogue: 'I'm not sure which...to use' (expressing uncertainty) — 'I'm not sure which service to use.' / 'Would you like...or...?' (offering a choice) — 'Would you like standard or express?' These structures help in real-life situations at banks, post offices, shops, or hospitals. Notice the indirect structure: 'I'm not sure + question word + to + infinitive'.

translationVi:
Nhân viên: Chào buổi sáng! Tôi có thể giúp gì cho bạn hôm nay?
Khách hàng: Xin chào, tôi cần gửi bưu kiện này cho gia đình ở Việt Nam. Tôi không chắc nên dùng dịch vụ nào.
Nhân viên: Không sao, tôi có thể giúp bạn. Bạn có thể đặt lên cân cho tôi được không?
Khách hàng: Được chứ. Đây ạ.
Nhân viên: Được rồi, nặng một phẩy hai kilogram. Bạn muốn dùng bưu chính quốc tế thông thường hay chuyển phát nhanh?
Khách hàng: Sự khác biệt là gì?
Nhân viên: Thông thường mất khoảng mười đến mười bốn ngày làm việc và giá hai mươi hai đô. Chuyển phát nhanh đến trong ba đến năm ngày làm việc và giá ba mươi tám đô.
Khách hàng: Tôi muốn nó đến trước cuối tháng, vậy tôi sẽ chọn chuyển phát nhanh.
Nhân viên: Lựa chọn tốt. Tôi cần bạn điền vào tờ khai hải quan này. Chỉ cần viết mô tả ngắn gọn về nội dung và giá trị ước tính.
Khách hàng: Chủ yếu là quần áo và một ít socola. Có lẽ trị giá khoảng bốn mươi đô tất cả.
Nhân viên: Hoàn hảo. Ghi vào đây nhé. Và có gì dễ vỡ, chất lỏng hay thiết bị dùng pin ở bên trong không?
Khách hàng: Không, không có gì như vậy.
Nhân viên: Tốt. Trong khi tôi xử lý cái này, bạn có muốn mua tem không?
Khách hàng: Vâng. Tôi có thể mua một tập mười con tem cho thư địa phương không?
Nhân viên: Tất nhiên. Mười hai đô cho tem và ba mươi tám đô cho bưu kiện — tổng cộng năm mươi đô. Tiền mặt hay thẻ?
Khách hàng: Thẻ ạ. Đây rồi. Cảm ơn bạn đã giúp đỡ!
Nhân viên: Không có gì! Bưu kiện của bạn sẽ đến trong ba đến năm ngày làm việc. Chúc bạn một ngày tốt lành!

questions.prompt: Where is the customer sending the parcel?
questions.explanation: 'I need to send this parcel to my family in Vietnam.'
questions.hint: He mentions a specific country and says 'family'.

questions.prompt: How much does the parcel weigh?
questions.hint: The weight is given by the clerk after placing it on the scales.

questions.prompt: How long does standard international post take?
questions.explanation: 'Standard takes about ten to fourteen business days.'
questions.hint: The clerk explains both options — standard is the slower one.

questions.prompt: How much does the express service cost?
questions.explanation: 'Express gets there in three to five business days and it's thirty-eight dollars.'
questions.hint: The clerk gives both prices when comparing the two options.

questions.prompt: Why does the customer choose express?
options: Because he wants it to arrive before the end of the month
Because express is the only option available
questions.explanation: 'I'd like it to arrive before the end of the month, so I'll go with express.'
questions.hint: He gives a time-related reason for his choice.

questions.prompt: What does the customer need to fill in?
questions.explanation: 'I'll need you to fill in this customs declaration form.'
questions.hint: This form is required for international parcels.
The customer lists two types of items.

questions.prompt: What is the approximate value of the parcel's contents?
questions.hint: He gives an estimate for the customs form.

questions.prompt: Which of these does the clerk ask if is inside the parcel?
questions.explanation: 'Is there anything fragile, liquid, or battery-operated inside?'

questions.prompt: How many stamps does the customer buy?
questions.explanation: 'Can I get a book of ten stamps for local letters?'
questions.hint: He asks for a specific number in a 'book'.

questions.prompt: How much do the stamps cost?
questions.hint: The clerk separates the costs of stamps and parcel.

questions.explanation: The customer says 'Card please' when asked 'Cash or card?'
questions.hint: The customer chooses between two options.

cloze.template:
Clerk: Good morning! How can I help you today?
Customer: I need to send this {{b1}} to my family in Vietnam. I'm not sure which service to use.
Clerk: Can you pop it on the {{b2}} for me?
Customer: Sure. Here you go.
Clerk: It weighs one point two kilograms. Would you like standard international post or {{b3}}?
Customer: What's the difference?
Clerk: Standard takes about ten to fourteen {{b4}} days and costs twenty-two dollars. Express gets there in three to five days and it's thirty-eight dollars.
Customer: I'd like it to arrive before the end of the {{b5}}, so I'll go with express.
Clerk: Good choice. I'll need you to fill in this customs {{b6}} form. Write a brief description of what's inside and the approximate {{b7}}.
Customer: It's mostly clothes and some chocolates. Probably worth about forty dollars {{b8}}.
Clerk: Is there anything {{b9}}, liquid, or battery-operated inside?
Customer: No, nothing like that.
Clerk: Would you like to buy any {{b10}} as well?
Customer: Yes. Can I get a book of ten stamps for local letters?
Clerk: That's twelve dollars for the stamps and thirty-eight for the parcel — {{b11}} dollars in total. Cash or {{b12}}?
Customer: Card please.

blanks.explanation: 'parcel' — a parcel is a wrapped package sent by post.
'scales' — scales are used to weigh parcels.
'express' — express is the faster, more expensive postal service.
'business' — business days exclude weekends and public holidays.
'month' — the customer wants it to arrive before the end of the month.
'declaration' — a customs declaration form lists the contents of an international parcel.
'value' — the approximate value is the estimated worth of the contents.
'fragile' — fragile means easily broken, requiring careful handling.
'stamps' — stamps are used to pay for postage on letters.
'fifty' — twelve for stamps plus thirty-eight for the parcel equals fifty dollars.
'card' — the customer chooses to pay by card.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sending parcels internationally has become easier with modern postal and courier services. However, many people now prefer to send digital gifts like gift cards or online subscriptions. Do you think physical gifts are more meaningful than digital ones? Why or why not?
